mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) directly increases aggregate labor income and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns higher. This relationship is persistent across economic conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:Nonfarm payrolls are a key labor market indicator closely watched by investors.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of months where PAYEMS increases month-over-month, or Rank IC of PAYEMS growth vs forward returns is below 0 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the federal funds rate from the near-zero lower bound signals a tightening monetary policy cycle, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations. The market reacts negatively to the first signs of rate increases.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% for the first time in at least 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate has been at 0.1% since December 2008, any increase would be significant.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) exit regime
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a FEDFUNDS rise above 0.25%, or the average return is not significantly negative (p>0.10 in one-sided t-test).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates synchronized expansion in both manufacturing and consumer sectors, producing robust economic momentum that strongly boosts equity returns over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Concurrent growth in production and consumption is a classic coincident indicator of economic expansion.
failure_condition: Over 3-year rolling window, the rank IC of the interaction term (product of standardized growth rates) with 5-day forward returns is less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE above 8%), declines in the unemployment rate signal meaningful labor market improvement that directly boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to above-average positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns after UNRATE declines are not significantly positive (mean < 0, p>0.05) in the high-unemployment regime, or the average return is less than the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2.5%, it signals an overheating economy and potential monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI is the most widely followed inflation measure.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of months when CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increase in PAYEMS and decrease in UNRATE indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts corporate profits and stock prices through higher consumption and reduced slack.
evidence_refs: fact:Joint labor market improvements are a strong coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The joint signal (PAYEMS growth + UNRATE decline) shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are below 800 thousand units, further declines indicate deepening weakness in the construction sector and broader economic headwinds, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts lead economic activity and are sensitive to interest rates.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in at least half of the months when HOUST declines in the low-HOUST regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) is a consistent leading indicator of corporate profits and economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over longer horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key measure of output and capacity utilization.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth has no positive correlation with 60-day SPX returns over any 5-year window.
